purpose: VS Code-style bottom panel with Terminal, Problems, Output, Debug Console, and Ports tabs. Supports up to 5 terminal tabs PER dashboard, drag-to-resize from the top edge, and embedded vs overlay rendering for IDE vs dashboard mode. Tracks per-dashboard terminal state in a single `termState` object keyed by dashboardId so PTYs survive dashboard switches; ALL dashboards' TerminalView components stay mounted with display:none when inactive (PTY+xterm processes never tear down on tab swap). Reads diagnostics from AppContext to badge the Problems tab and entries.length to badge Output.

exports.name: BottomPanel
exports.signature: { logs, activeFilter, onFilterChange, projectDir, dashboardId, onNavigate, embedded?: boolean }

gotchas: All dashboards' terminals stay mounted simultaneously — heavy memory use if many dashboards have multiple terminals open. The `display:none` toggle keeps PTY processes alive but does not release xterm/canvas resources.
Drag resize uses pointer capture + RAF throttling; failing to release pointer capture on unmount could leave the cursor stuck — `onUp` is invoked in cleanup to defend against this.
MAX_TERMINAL_TABS=5 silently caps further additions; the +button just disappears with no toast/error.
Per-dashboard term state is initialized lazily in an effect — first render of a new dashboard renders DEFAULT_TERM before the effect commits, so the first frame may flicker to the default.
patterns: Per-dashboard isolation via `termState[dashboardId]` map; effect ensures every dashboardId has a record before tabs render.
All panes mounted; only `display` toggles between flex/none — preserves scroll position and component state across tab switches.
Drag handle uses `dragStateRef` + `rafRef` (no React state during drag) to avoid re-render storms; `bottom-panel-resizing` body class disables hover effects across the app while dragging.
conventions: Terminal IDs (1, 2, ...) are local per-dashboard, not global. Tab labels follow `Terminal {id}`.
Constants DEFAULT_PANEL_HEIGHT, MIN_PANEL_HEIGHT, HEADER_HEIGHT defined at module scope.
